root_text.sn45.170:1.5: Imāsaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tissannaṁ taṇhānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe… ayaṁ ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvetabbo.
root_text.sn45.170:1.7: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn45.170:1.8: Imāsaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tissannaṁ taṇhānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe… ayaṁ ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvetabbo”ti.
root_text.sn45.170:2.4: Imāsaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tissannaṁ tasinānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe….
root_text.sn45.170:2.8: Imāsaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tissannaṁ tasinānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe… ayaṁ ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvetabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn45.170:1.3: Craving for sensual pleasures, craving for existence, and craving for nonexistence.
translation_text.sn45.170:1.5: The noble eightfold path should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these three cravings.
translation_text.sn45.170:1.6: What is the noble eightfold path?
translation_text.sn45.170:1.7: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn45.170:1.8: This is the noble eightfold path that should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these three cravings.”
translation_text.sn45.170:2.3: Thirst for sensual pleasures, thirst to continue existence, and thirst to end existence.
translation_text.sn45.170:2.4: For the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these three thirsts …
translation_text.sn45.170:2.5: … which culminates in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn45.170:2.6: … whose objective, destination, and culmination is freedom from death.
translation_text.sn45.170:2.7: … which slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn45.170:2.8: The noble eightfold path should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these three thirsts.”
